title: EEG: Simon Conflict in Parkinson's
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 28 Parkinson's disease patients and 28 matched controls performing a Simon conflict task with cost of conflict reinforcement manipulation. Parkinson's patients were tested twice within one week under both ON and OFF medication conditions, while controls participated in a single session. The study investigates neural correlates of conflict processing and decision-making in Parkinson's disease using event-related potentials and behavioral measures.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during a Simon conflict task adapted from established protocols. Participants completed the task in a single session (controls) or two sessions separated by one week (Parkinson's patients, tested ON and OFF medication). Accelerometer data were simultaneously recorded from the most tremor-affected hand in three dimensions (X, Y, Z). Data were collected circa 2015 at the Cognitive Rhythms and Computation Lab at the University of New Mexico.
